Formic acid (CHzOz. Problem 4.5) is responsible for the sting of some types of ants_
The rules for drawing Lewis electron dot structures have been met: 1) use all the valence electrons (18 in this case), and 2) meet the octet rule for elements in groups 4A through 8A (in this case oxygen and carbon, hydrogen follows the duet rule):

PROBLEM 4.8
Glyphosate is the most widely used weed killer and the active ingredient in Roundup and other lawn products. (a) Add lone pairs around all Nand 0 atoms to complete octets. (b) How many electrons surround phosphorus in the given structure? (c) Which atoms in glyphosate do not follow the octet rule? Cz H p NO 5 C 1 2e" 3 A A :0 + {e Se" H ~N_ O_H 8 5 30 e H_0: H H H P Se" + glyphosate 60 e Valence
b) There are 5 bonds around the phosphorus, which indicates 10 electrons instead of 8 electrons. Phosphorus and sulfur sometimes do not follow the octet rule because their valence electrons are in the 3rd period and the 3rd period has d orbitals. The empty d orbitals in their 3rd periods can be used for bonding: They are exceptions to the octet rule: c) The phosphorus in glyphosate does not follow the octet rule:
